setting.note: The Palais Garnier is real, and Leroux insisted his story was too. He maps the building in precise detail: the auditorium and its great chandelier, Box Five on the grand tier, the dancers' foyer, the roof beneath Apollo's lyre, and the cellars below. The lake is real as well, a cistern built to hold back the groundwater that Leroux turned into the Phantom's underground lake.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Gaston Leroux, first published 1910; the author died in 1927.
rights.translation: English translation by Alexander Teixeira de Mattos (1911); he died in 1921, so the translation is public domain in the US and UK/EU.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the US and UK/EU: Leroux died 1927, de Mattos 1921.
rights.attribution: Gaston Leroux, The Phantom of the Opera (1910), trans. Alexander Teixeira de Mattos. Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The scarlet of the Red Death costume the Phantom wears to the masked ball, the one splash of colour in a book of shadows. A deep red that clears WCAG AA on paper.
